channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Microsoft Employees Protest AI Technology Sales to Israeli Military
item.description: Pro-Palestinian protest at Microsoft's 50th anniversary celebration highlights concerns over tech industry's work in Israel
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: My dog recognizes the sounds a Waymo car makes
item.description: Most of us know the general (albeit simplified) story: Russian physiologist Ivan Pavlov used a stimulus—like a metronome—around the dogs he was studying, and soon, the hounds would start to salivate. They had learned that the sound meant food was coming.
item.dc:creator: Jessica Bursztynsky, Fast Company

item.title: This mini PC has a 7-inch display that can apparently run Windows and an AMD Ryzen 9 CPU, but there's even better options to be had
item.description: The compact yet powerful Kingdel Mini PC delivers desktop-level performance in a portable package with a built-in touchscreen display.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: At 50, Microsoft highlights AI and Copilot as the company’s future
item.description: At a special 50th anniversary event on Friday, Microsoft executives reflected on the company’s storied past and on how it’s now reinventing itself for an AI-focused future.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Google Cloud Next ’25: What to expect
item.description: Google Cloud Next ’25, which runs from April 9-11 in Las Vegas ,will showcase the latest advancements and future directions of Google Cloud Platform and cloud computing in general.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: AI vs. pro gambler's $1 million March Madness bet coming down to Duke and Houston
item.description: SAN ANTONIO (AP) — The $1 million March Madness wager between a pro gambler and an artificial-intelligence site will be decided by the winner of the Duke-Houston game in the Final Four. The Vegas bookies project the gambler, who picked 5 1/2-point favorite Duke, should win.
item.dc:creator: EDDIE PELLS, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Windows 11 is getting a very handy change to the taskbar, as Microsoft takes a leaf from Apple’s Mac playbook
item.description: Microsoft could be gunning for big changes to the Windows 11 desktop, as a nifty taskbar tweak emerges following rumored Start menu revamp.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Are modern smartphone designs boring? We asked 1,500 people, and the results are damning
item.description: We asked 1,500 people whether they think modern smartphone designs are boring, and the results paint a pessimistic picture.
item.dc:creator: axel.metz@futurenet.com (Axel Metz), TechRadar

item.title: Marvel Rivals Season 2 release date, Emma Frost, and upcoming changes
item.description: Here's when to expect Marvel Rivals Season 2, as well as some info on the new Heroes Emma Frost and Ultron. Also, a look at upcoming changes to Team Ups.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Top 15 Microsoft Azure certifications to expedite your IT career
item.description: With companies increasingly moving their data to cloud, there’s an extensive need for more professionals with sound understanding and expertise of cloud technology. Microsoft’s cloud offering, Azure, ranks among the top in the industry.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Cobas Asset Management moderniza su atención al cliente con un asistente virtual impulsado por IA
item.description: Cobas Asset Management ha puesto en marcha un nuevo proyecto con vistas a mejorar la experiencia de usuario en su web y en su área privada gracias a la incorporación de un asistente virtual.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Enterprises are getting worse at multicloud
item.description: In the initial years of multicloud adoption, enterprises took cautious, calculated steps to build and manage infrastructures that spanned multiple cloud providers. The objective was flexibility, performance optimization, and risk mitigation.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Cómo empezar a desarrollar una estrategia equilibrada de gobernanza de la IA
item.description: La mejor defensa es un buen ataque, ¿verdad? Cuando se trata de la gobernanza de la IA, las organizaciones necesitan ambas cosas.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: TikTok Notes is shutting down as Lemon8 steps in
item.description: TikTok is shutting down TikTok Notes—wait, you didn’t even know it existed? Well, that explains a lot. TikTok Notes, the platform’s short-lived attempt to take on Instagram (just as Instagram Reels was built to mimic TikTok), is officially being retired.
item.dc:creator: Eve Upton-Clark, Fast Company

item.title: Brown University to see half a billion in federal funding halted by Trump administration
item.description: A White House official says the Trump administration is planning to halt more than half a billion dollars in contracts and grants awarded to Brown University.
item.dc:creator: Cheyanne Mumphrey And Seung Min Kim, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: How Elon Musk’s political gambit could tarnish his legacy at Tesla
item.description: Tech leaders often brand themselves as “disruptors”—and few fit that label more snugly than Elon Musk. In the three months since joining Donald Trump in the White House following Trump’s election, Musk has certainly disrupted Washington.
item.dc:creator: Chris Stokel-Walker, Fast Company

item.title: Microsoft founder Bill Gates reflects upon a 50-year-old computer code that reshaped technology
item.description: Even as he grows older, Microsoft founder Bill Gates still fondly remembers the catalytic computer code he wrote 50 years ago that opened up a new frontier in technology.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL LIEDTKE, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Tariff war throws building of data centers into disarray
item.description: Enterprise IT leaders are facing a double-whammy of uncertainties complicating their data center building decisions: The ever-changing realities of genAI strategies, and the back-and-forth nature of the current tariff wars pushed by the United States. “This is obviously a fluid situation.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Microsoft urges Office users to upgrade to 365
item.description: Microsoft is urging Office 2016 and 2019 customers to upgrade to Microsoft 365 before support ends Oct. 14, but analysts said viable alternatives are available outside Microsoft’s walled garden.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: How to accelerate digital transformation
item.description: Readers of CIO, Computerworld, CSO, InfoWorld, and Network World this week sought to learn how corporate culture can affect digital transformation, wanted to know more about the rise of cloud-based ERP, and had questions about desktop-based office software.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: New Relic simplifies Kubernetes performance monitoring
item.description: New Relic this week announced a new capability in its Intelligent Observability Platform that the company says will enable engineering teams to deploy an eBPF-powered agent to automatically discover and monitor all applications running on Kubernetes clusters.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: A behind-the-scenes look at Nintendo Switch 2
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Nintendo's Switch 2 is bigger and better than its predecessor of eight years past as the video game developer pushes to bring its brand into “a new era" where social experiences are at the forefront.
item.dc:creator: RYAN KRYSKA, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Informatica readies new Claire Copilot capabilities for IDMC
item.description: Enterprise data management vendor Informatica is adding new capabilities to Claire Copilot, the generative AI assistant in its Intelligent Data Management Cloud (IDMC).
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Why no one is challenging Trump's executive order that keeps TikTok running
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — After TikTok was banned in the United States earlier this year, President Donald Trump gave the platform a reprieve, barreling past a law that was passed in Congress and upheld unanimously by the Supreme Court that said the ban was necessary for national security.
item.dc:creator: SARAH PARVINI and MARK SHERMAN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: 15 ways to speed up Windows 11
item.description: Windows 11 does a lot under the hood to speed up a PC’s performance. The same PC will generally run faster on Windows 11 than Windows 10. But no matter how zippy your Windows 11 may be, it can run faster.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Making Python faster won’t be easy, but it’ll be worth it
item.description: Want to make a Python user grind their teeth? Just recite three words: Python is slow. In many of the ways that matter, it’s true.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Trump to hold a meeting on possible investors to buy TikTok with possible ban at stake
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — President Donald Trump will hold a Wednesday meeting with aides about possible investors who could buy a stake in TikTok, a deal that could potentially stop the social media site from being banned in the United States.
item.dc:creator: SARAH PARVINI and JOSH BOAK, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Detroit production of Mozart opera turns its female characters into automatons
item.description: DETROIT (AP) — Audiences at the Detroit Opera House expecting their performance of “Cosi fan tutte" to begin with the overture may be surprised to hear instead a product launch from a tech company CEO.
item.dc:creator: MIKE SILVERMAN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Emergency Chrome security update amid cyber espionage threats
item.description: Tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson reports that researchers have uncovered a Chrome vulnerability used in a cyber espionage campaign.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: 23andMe customer DNA ‘up for grabs’ amid bankruptcy: Attorney
item.description: With a saliva sample, DNA-testing companies like 23andMe provide a profile of a person’s genetic heritage.
item.dc:creator: Michael Ramsey, NewsNation
